'''Jeremiah Simbiken''' (born 17 August 2000) is a [[Papua New Guinea|Papua New Guinean]] professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|sr}} forward for the [[London Broncos]] in the [[RFL Championship]] and {{rlnt|Papua New Guinea}} at international level.

He previously played for the [[Redcliffe Dolphins]] and [[Norths Devils]] in the [[QLD Cup]], as well as being contracted to the [[Dolphins (NRL)|Dolphins]] in the [[NRL]]. Simbiken has also played for the [[Castleford Tigers]] in the [[Super League]].

==Background==
Simbiken was born in [[Goroka]], Papua New Guinea but is originally from [[Wewak]] in the [[East Sepik Province]] of Papua New Guinea. He moved to [[Canberra]], Australia when he was 10 years old.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Tambua |first=Parker |date=2 September 2022 |title=Simbiken proud of his sons |url=https://www.thenational.com.pg/simbiken-proud-of-his-sons/ |access-date=9 February 2025 |website=The National}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |date=29 August 2022 |title=NRL bound |url=https://postcourier.com.pg/nrl-bound/ |access-date=9 February 2025 |website=Post Courier}}</ref>

Simbiken was a member of the [[Canberra Raiders]] junior system.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2 December 2022 |title=Who's Who in 22, Sport: Jeremiah Simbiken |url=https://www.moretondaily.com.au/news/whos-who-in-22-sport-jeremiah-simbiken |access-date=11 February 2025 |website=Moreton Daily}}</ref>

==Club career==
===Redcliffe Dolphins===
In the [[2022 Queensland Cup]] state competition, Simbiken debuted for the [[Redcliffe Dolphins]] and scored a total of thirteen tries in sixteen matches.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Pritchard |first=Matt |title=Jeremiah Simbiken |url=https://nothingbutleague.com/player/jeremiah-simbiken/ |access-date=9 February 2025 |website=Nothing But League}}</ref>

=== Dolphins ===
On 25 August 2022, Simbiken signed a three-year deal with the [[Dolphins (NRL)]] club, seeing him hold development contract status with them from 2023.<ref>{{Cite web |date=25 August 2022 |title=From the verge of walking away, Simbiken earns NRL contract |url=https://www.dolphinsnrl.com.au/news/2022/08/25/from-the-verge-of-walking-away-simbiken-earns-nrl-contract/ |access-date=9 February 2025 |website=The Dolphins}}</ref>

=== Norths Devils ===
In 2024, Simbiken won the [[Queensland Cup]] starting in the second row for the [[Norths Devils]] in their Grand Final win against the [[Redcliffe Dolphins]].<ref>{{Cite web |date=22 September 2024 |title=Devils v Dolphins |url=https://www.qrl.com.au/draw/qrl-premiership/2024/grand-final/game-1/ |access-date=9 February 2025 |website=Queensland Rugby League |archive-date=14 September 2024 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240914033621/https://www.qrl.com.au/draw/qrl-premiership/2024/grand-final/game-1/ |url-status=dead }}</ref> Two weeks later, he helped the Devils win the [[NRL State Championship]], scoring the opening try against the [[Newtown Jets]].<ref>{{Cite web |date=6 October 2024 |title=Jets v Devils |url=https://www.nrl.com/draw/nrl-state-championship/2024/nrl-state-championship/jets-v-devils/ |access-date=6 October 2024 |website=National Rugby League}}</ref>

=== Castleford Tigers ===
On 4 December 2024, the [[Castleford Tigers]] announced the signing of Simbiken from the [[2025 Castleford Tigers season|2025 season]].<ref>{{Cite web |last=Olawumi |first=Ben |date=4 December 2024 |title=Castleford Tigers snap up Papua New Guinea international forward |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/castleford-tigers-snap-up-papua-new-guinea-international-forward |access-date=4 December 2024 |website=Love Rugby League}}</ref> He was assigned squad number 11,<ref>{{Cite web |last=Darbyshire |first=Drew |date=6 December 2024 |title=Castleford Tigers reveal 2025 squad numbers as new-look spine formed |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/castleford-tigers-reveal-2025-squad-numbers-as-new-look-spine-formed |access-date=9 February 2024 |website=Love Rugby League}}</ref> and scored a try on debut on 9 February against [[Bradford Bulls|Bradford]] in the [[2025 Challenge Cup|Challenge Cup]].<ref>{{Cite web |date=9 February 2025 |title=Bradford upset Cas as Salford & Giants go through |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/ce8jm7y7m34o |access-date=9 February 2025 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref> He scored his first [[Super League]] try against [[Leeds Rhinos]] in round 3,<ref>{{Cite web |last=Lanigan |first=Adam |date=2 March 2025 |title=Leeds cruise to victory over struggling Castleford |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cn4ymrleyjeo |access-date=19 May 2025 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref> though was also given a two-match suspension for dangerous contact.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Walker |first=Callum |date=5 March 2025 |title=Castleford Tigers' Danny McGuire gives verdict on Jeremiah Simbiken and Tom Holroyd bans |url=https://www.totalrl.com/castleford-tigers-danny-mcguire-gives-verdict-on-jeremiah-simbiken-and-tom-holroyd-bans/ |access-date=19 May 2025 |website=TotalRL}}</ref> In April, Simbiken and his Papua New Guinean teammates met with [[King Charles III]] in support of the charitable organisation [[Mission Aviation Fellowship]].<ref>{{Cite web |last=Cook |first=Sam |date=3 April 2025 |title=Castleford Tigers quartet make royal visit to meet King Charles III |url=https://www.seriousaboutrl.com/castleford-tigers-quartet-make-royal-visit-to-meet-king-charles-iii-103185/ |access-date=11 October 2025 |website=Serious About Rugby League}}</ref> In the closing stages of Castleford's round 9 win against [[Huddersfield Giants|Huddersfield]], Simbiken suffered a knee injury and was helped from the field.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Shaw |first=Matthew |date=26 April 2025 |title=Castleford Tigers' double injury blow outlined with Jordan Dezaria stance given |url=https://www.alloutrugbyleague.co.uk/news/castleford-tigers-double-injury-blow-1116116 |access-date=19 May 2025 |website=All Out Rugby League}}</ref> Initial scans indicated he would be ruled out for 12 weeks,<ref>{{Cite web |last=Bower |first=Aaron |date=1 May 2025 |title=Castleford Tigers hunting more new signings after fresh long-term injury blow |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/castleford-tigers-hunting-more-new-signings-after-fresh-long-term-injury-blow |access-date=19 May 2025 |website=Love Rugby League}}</ref> however following a successful operation this prognosis was improved to 8–10 weeks.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Chapman Coombe |first=Louis |date=15 May 2025 |title=Danny McGuire issues Jeremiah Simbiken update with new timeframe revealed |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/danny-mcguire-issues-jeremiah-simbiken-update-with-new-timeframe-revealed |access-date=19 May 2025 |website=Love Rugby League}}</ref> After returning to the team in July, Simbiken received three suspensions for minor offences under the new 'penalty points' disciplinary format,<ref>{{Cite web |last=Tomlinson |first=Daniel |date=21 July 2025 |title=Two Super League players banned in latest disciplinary verdict |url=https://www.alloutrugbyleague.co.uk/news/two-super-league-players-banned-1280695 |access-date=11 October 2025 |website=All Out Rugby League}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Tomlinson |first=Daniel |date=11 August 2025 |title=Castleford Tigers forward suspended as St Helens' George Delaney learns fate for high tackle |url=https://www.alloutrugbyleague.co.uk/news/castleford-tigers-forward-suspended-st-1322485 |access-date=11 October 2025 |website=All Out Rugby League}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Cook |first=Sam |date=12 September 2025 |title=Chris Chester 'disillusioned' with Super League disciplinary system as Castleford boss becomes the latest to speak out |url=https://www.seriousaboutrl.com/rugby-league-news-chris-chester-disillusioned-with-super-league-disciplinary-system-as-castleford-boss-becomes-the-latest-to-speak-out-108305/ |access-date=11 October 2025 |website=Serious About Rugby League}}</ref> and an elbow injury sustained in the round 25 win over [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield]] ended his season early.<ref name="YEP">{{Cite web |last=Smith |first=Peter |date=11 September 2025 |title=Castleford Tigers news: overseas player's exit confirmed as boss frustrated with disciplinary |url=https://www.yorkshireeveningpost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/castleford-tigers-news-overseas-players-exit-confirmed-as-boss-frustrated-with-disciplinary-5314085 |access-date=11 October 2025 |website=Yorkshire Evening Post}}</ref> Despite impressing in his first Super League season, Simbiken opted to depart at the conclusion of his contract.<ref name="YEP" /><ref>{{Cite web |last=Chapman Coombe |first=Louis |date=11 September 2025 |title=Castleford Tigers coach confirms overseas star's exit as Sam Wood link to Hull FC addressed |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/castleford-tigers-coach-confirms-overseas-stars-exit-as-sam-wood-link-to-hull-fc-addressed |access-date=28 September 2025 |website=Love Rugby League}}</ref>

===London Broncos===
On 10 November 2025 it was reported that he had signed for the [[London Broncos]] in the [[RFL Championship]].<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=10 Nov 2025|author=Aaron Bower|title=London Broncos snap up two more Papua New Guinea stars including ex-Castleford man|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/london-broncos-snap-up-two-more-papua-new-guinea-stars-including-ex-castleford-man}}</ref>
[[File:Jeremiah Simbiken.jpg|thumb|upright|Simbiken warming up for the London Broncos in 2026]]

== International career ==
In October 2022, Simbiken was named in the [[2021_Men's_Rugby_League_World_Cup_squads#Papua_New_Guinea|Papua New Guinea squad]] for the [[2021 Rugby League World Cup]] in England.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Darbyshire |first=Drew |date=4 October 2022 |title=Full list of every squad at the Rugby League World Cup 2021 |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/rugby-league-world-cup-2021-squad-lists/ |access-date=9 February 2025 |website=Love Rugby League}}</ref> He made his international debut for [[Papua New Guinea national rugby league team|PNG]] on 31 October 2022 against [[Wales national rugby league team|Wales]],<ref>{{Cite web |last=Pope |first=Bruce |date=31 October 2022 |title=Rugby League World Cup: Papua New Guinea 36-0 Wales - Kumuls seal quarter-final against England |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/63464967 |access-date=11 February 2025 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Tambua |first=Parker |date=31 October 2022 |title=Duo to debut against Wales |url=https://www.thenational.com.pg/duo-to-debut-against-wales/ |access-date=9 February 2025 |website=The National}}</ref> and also played in the quarter final against {{Rlnt|England}}.<ref>{{Cite web |date=5 November 2022 |title=England v Papua New Guinea |url=https://www.nrl.com/draw/rugby-league-world-cup/2022/quarter-finals/game-2/ |access-date=11 February 2025 |website=National Rugby League}}</ref>

Simbiken was included in the [[2024 Rugby League Pacific Championships squads#Papua New Guinea|Kumuls squad]] for the [[2024 Rugby League Pacific Championships|2024 Pacific Championships]] in October 2024. He featured in all three of PNG's matches, playing against {{Rlnt|Fiji}} and the {{Rlnt|Cook Islands}} in the group stage and {{Rlnt|New Zealand}} in the promotion play-off.<ref>{{Cite web |date=19 October 2024 |title=Fiji Bati v PNG Kumuls |url=https://www.nrl.com/draw/pacific-championships/2024/week-1/fiji-bati-v-png-kumuls/ |access-date=11 February 2025 |website=National Rugby League}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |date=3 November 2024 |title=PNG Kumuls v Cook Islands Aitu |url=https://www.nrl.com/draw/pacific-championships/2024/week-3/png-kumuls-v-cook-islands-aitu/ |access-date=11 February 2025 |website=National Rugby League}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |date=10 November 2024 |title=Kiwis v PNG Kumuls |url=https://www.nrl.com/draw/pacific-championships/2024/final/game-2/ |access-date=11 February 2025 |website=National Rugby League}}</ref>
